title: Nanoparticles for use in treating viral infections
abstract: The present disclosure relates to the treatment of viral infections comprising administering a population of polymeric nanoparticles comprising salinomycin to a subject in need thereof.
claims: Claims 1. A method of mitigating death or promoting survival of a population of cells with a viral infection, the method comprising administering to the population of cells a therapeutically effective amount of a pharmaceutical composition comprising polymeric nanoparticles, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise salinomycin. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the population of cells are human cells. 3. A method of treating a viral infection in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a pharmaceutical composition comprising polymeric nanoparticles to the subject, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise salinomycin. 4. A method of improving a symptom associated with a viral infection in a subject in need thereof, the method comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a pharmaceutical composition comprising polymeric nanoparticles to the subject, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise salinomycin. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the symptom associated with the viral infection is selected from the group consisting of fever, cough, shortness of breath, pain or pressure in the chest, confusion, bluish lips or face, rash, conjunctivitis, pneumonia, bronchitis, runny nose, sneezing, chills, exacerbated asthma, acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS), RNAaemia, acute cardiac injury, shock, myalgia, fatigue, sputum production, rusty colored sputum, bloody sputum, swelling of lymph nodes, middle ear infection, joint pain, wheezing, headache, hemoptysis, diarrhea, dyspnea, redness, swelling or edema, pain, loss of function, organ dysfunction, multi-organ system failure, acute kidney injury, malnutrition, sepsis, hypotension, hypertension, hypothermia, hypoxemia, leukocytosis, leukopenia, lymphopenia, thrombocytopenia, nasal congestion, sore throat, unwillingness to drink, convulsions, ongoing vomiting, abdominal pain, secondary infection, cytokine release syndrome, and multi-organ failure. 6. A method of reducing the probability of viral infection in a subject in need thereof, tbe metbod comprising administering a therapeutically effective amount of a pharmaceutical composition comprising polymeric nanoparticles to the subject, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise salinomycin. 7. The method of any one of claims 3-6, wherein the subject is a human. 8. The method of any one of claims 3-7, wherein the pharmaceutical composition is administered intradermally, intramuscularly, intraperitoneally, intravenously, subcutaneously, intranasally, epidurally, sublingually, intracerebrally, intravaginally, transdermally, rectally, topically, or via inhalation. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the viral infection is caused by a coronavirus. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the coronavirus is a betacoronavirus. 11. The method of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the coronavirus is selected from the group consisting of human coronavirus OC43 (HCoV-OC43), severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus (SARS-CoV), human coronavirus HKU1 (HCoV-HKUl), Middle East respiratory syndrome-related coronavirus (MERS-CoV), and severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2). 12. The method of any one of claims 9-11, wherein the coronavirus is SARS-CoV-2. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the viral infection is caused by an Alphavirus. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the Alphavirus is Chikungunya virus. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise a poly(lactic acid)-poly(ethylene glycol)-poly (propylene glycol)-poly(ethylene glycol) (PLA- PEG-PPG-PEG) tetra block copolymer. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the PLA-PEG-PPG-PEG tetra-block copolymer is formed from chemical conjugation of PEG-PPG-PEG tri-block copolymer with PLA. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the molecular weight of PLA is between about 10,000 and about 100,000 daltons. 18. The method of claim 16 or 17, wherein the molecular weight of PLA is between about 20,000 and about 90,000 daltons. 19. The method of any one of claims 15-18, wherein the molecular weight of PLA is between about 30,000 and about 80,000 daltons. 20. The method of any one of claims 15-19, wherein the molecular weight of PEG-PPG-PEG is between about 8,000 daltons and about 18,000 daltons. 21. The method of any one of claims 15-20, wherein the molecular weight of PEG-PPG-PEG is between about 12,000 daltons and about 17,000 daltons. 22. The method of claim 19 or 21, wherein the molecular weight of PLA in the copolymer is between about 30,000 and about 80,000 daltons and the molecular weight of PEG-PPG-PEG is between about 12,000 daltons and about 17,000 daltons. 23. The method of any one of claims 15-22, wherein the average diameter of the polymeric nanoparticles is between about 70 nm and about 120 nm. 24. The method of any one of claims 15-23, wherein the average diameter of the polymeric nanoparticles is between about 90 nm and about 110 nm. 25. The method of any one of claims 15-24, wherein the average diameter of the polymeric nanoparticles is between about 95 nm and about 105 nm. 26. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the polymeric nanoparticles comprise a di-block copolymer methoxy-poly(ethylene glycol)-poly(lactic acid) (m-PEG-PLA) and/or a penta-block copolymer poly(lactic acid)-poly(ethylene glycol)-poly(propylene glycol)- poly(ethylene glycol)-poly(lactic acid) (PLA-PEG-PPG-PEG-PLA). 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the copolymer has an average molecular weight in the range of about 5,000-30,000 g/mol. 28. The method of claim 26 or 27, wherein the polymeric nanoparticle has an average size of about 40-150 nm. 29. The method of any one of claims 15-28, wherein the biodegradable polymeric nanoparticle is substantially free of emulsifier. 30. The method of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the biodegradable polymeric nanoparticle further comprises an external emulsifier present in a weight of about 0.5% to 5% of the total weight of the polymer. 31. The method of any one of claims 1-30, wherein the biodegradable polymeric nanoparticle further comprises a stabilizer. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the stabilizer is selected from a group consisting of mannose, beta-lactose, trehalose, sodium cholate, and glucose. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the stabilizer is glucose. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the glucose is present in a weight of about 5% to about 50% of the total weight of the polymer.
